messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] windows binaries missing in 2.2 and 2.3, not in check results either?
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] windows binaries missing in 2.2 and 2.3, not in check results either?
messages.body_plain:
Dear all:

just wanted to draw your attention to the fact that Windows builds seem 
to be missing from both 2.2 and 2.3 check results and Windows binaries 
also missing at the pages of individual packages.

I do not know maybe the previous Windows build in the same branch is 
still accessible with the biocLite(), cannot check as have no Windows 
box, but links to binaries are missing. I did not go through all the 
package pages, but checked several at random. This occurs also for 
R-only packages, thus not a problem of C code compilation.

Windows build server down?

Best,
Oleg

-- 
Dr Oleg Sklyar * EBI-EMBL, Cambridge CB10 1SD, UK

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] windows binaries missing in 2.2 and 2.3, not in check results either?
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] windows binaries missing in 2.2 and 2.3, not in check results either?
messages.body_plain:
Thanks Oleg -- this came up in an earlier thread today. Some vigorous 
spring cleaning in preparation for the release was a little too 
effective. The windows binaries are being built as we speak, and should 
be back on line soon.

Sorry for the inconvenience.

Martin

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] windows binaries missing in 2.2 and 2.3, not in check results either?
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] windows binaries missing in 2.2 and 2.3, not in check results either?
messages.body_plain:
The updated BioC 2.2 Windows binary packages, along with the source 
packages and Mac OS X binary packages, are now available from 
http://bioconductor.org/packages/2.2/bioc. You can obtain them, as 
usual, through the biocLite function when using R 2.7.0.


Cheers,
Patrick
